comparisons.title: RAG vs CAG: Choosing the Right AI Architecture for 2026
comparisons.description: A technical comparison of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) vs Cache-Augmented Generation (CAG) for personal and enterprise knowledge bases.
comparisons.verdict: Use CAG for static datasets under 1M tokens (Obsidian vaults, Docs). Use RAG for massive, frequently changing dynamic data.

comparisons.title: Bun vs Node.js for Next.js Monorepos
comparisons.description: Analyzing runtime performance, build speeds, and developer experience for multi-app monorepos in 2026.
comparisons.verdict: Bun is the clear winner for new Next.js monorepos due to its 10x faster CI and unified workspace management.

blueprints.title: Next.js + MCP Architecture for Agentic AI Tools
blueprints.description: A battle-tested blueprint for building AI agents that interact with local and cloud data via the Model Context Protocol.
blueprints.problem: AI agents lack real-time access to fragmented tool states and local file systems.
blueprints.solution: Implementing a stateless MCP server bridge that allows LLMs to 'walk' data graphs instead of relying on static chunks.

blueprints.title: React + Zod: Zero-Drift Type Architecture
blueprints.description: How to synchronize types from the database to the frontend with 100% safety using shared Zod schemas.
blueprints.problem: Mismatched types between API and Frontend cause 40% of production runtime errors.
blueprints.solution: Centralizing schema definitions in a shared monorepo package, using Zod for both runtime validation and TypeScript inference.
